title: sideritis hirsuta subsp. vulgaris
intro: Sideritis hirsuta subsp. vulgaris is a plant species that belongs to the Sideritis genus, commonly known as mountain tea or ironwort, a type of flowering plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Sideritis hirsuta subsp. vulgaris include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Sideritis hirsuta subsp. vulgaris can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for its medicinal and health benefits.
